source.submodule: Bitwise Operators - 2 (NOT, LEFT and Right Shifts)

topicTags: Bitwise Operators - 2 (NOT, LEFT and Right Shifts)

content.problemStatement:
The Signed Data Types can represent positive as well as negative numbers. 
The Unsigned Data Types can only represent positive numbers.

To store negative numbers the Leftmost Bit in Signed Data Types is reserved for sign. \
If Leftmost is $0$, the number is positive and if it's $1$ then the number is negative.

```
29 ->   00000000000000000000000000011101
-30 ->  11111111111111111111111111100010
        ^ Most Significant bit
```
The NOT operator also flips the sign of signed-bit in signed data types.  
The negative binary numbers are evaluated in the form of 2s compliment.  
To calculate the value of a negative number, the computer flips all the digits and then adds one.  
For example:  
To calculate the value of 11111111111111111111111111100010 we flip all the digits which gives 00000000000000000000000000011101 and we add one resulting in 11110 which is 30. Thus, the value of 11111111111111111111111111100010 is -30  

```
x = 29
~x = 11111111111111111111111111100010 (4294967266 unsigned data type)
```


### Task 
Run the code and check the output of the program. Also change the values of variables $X$ and $Y$ and check the output.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
